title: campanula velutina subsp. winkleri
intro: Campanula velutina subsp. winkleri, also known as Winkler's campanula, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the bellflower family (Campanulaceae). It is used in traditional medicine, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and promoting wound healing, making it a valuable remedy in herbal medicine. Campanula velutina subsp. winkleri can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in folk remedies for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties.
